steps.title: Rewrite the radical as a power
steps.content:
A seventh root is the same as raising to the $\frac{1}{7}$ power. The condition $x\gt1$ keeps the base positive, so the usual exponent rules apply directly:
$$\sqrt[7]{x^5}=(x^5)^{1/7}.$$

steps.title: Apply the power rule
steps.content:
Use $(x^a)^b=x^{ab}$, which means the exponent $5$ is multiplied by $\frac{1}{7}$:
$$(x^5)^{1/7}=x^{5/7}$$
The expression is $x^m$, so $m=\frac{5}{7}$. The answer is 5/7.
